subcommands.name: -version
subcommands.description: Display the compiler version

subcommands.name: -nowarn
subcommands.description: Suppress the compiler from displaying warnings during compilation

subcommands.name: -Werror
subcommands.description: Turn any warnings into a compilation error

subcommands.name: -verbose
subcommands.description: Enable verbose logging output which includes details of the compilation process

subcommands.name: -script
subcommands.description: Evaluate a Kotlin script file. When called with this option, the compiler executes the first Kotlin script (*.kts) file among the given arguments

subcommands.name: -help
subcommands.description: Display usage information and exit. Only standard options are shown. To show advanced options, use -X

subcommands.name: -X
subcommands.description: Display information about the advanced options and exit. These options are currently unstable: their names and behavior may be changed without notice

subcommands.name: -kotlin-home
subcommands.description: Specify a custom path to the Kotlin compiler used for the discovery of runtime libraries

subcommands.name: -P
subcommands.description: Pass an option to a Kotlin compiler plugin. Available plugins and their options are listed in the Tools > Compiler plugins section of the documentation

subcommands.name: -language-version
subcommands.description: Provide source compatibility with the specified version of Kotlin

subcommands.name: -api-version
subcommands.description: Allow using declarations only from the specified version of Kotlin bundled libraries

subcommands.name: -progressive
subcommands.description: Enable the progressive mode for the compiler. In the progressive mode, deprecations and bug fixes for unstable code take effect immediately, instead of going through a graceful migration cycle. Code written in the progressive mode is backward compatible; however, code written in a non-progressive mode may cause compilation errors in the progressive mode

subcommands.name: -opt-in
subcommands.description: Enable usages of API that requires opt-in with a requirement annotation specified by the given fully qualified name

subcommands.name: -classpath
subcommands.description: Search for class files in the specified paths. Separate elements of the classpath with system path separators (; on Windows, : on macOS/Linux). The classpath can contain file and directory paths, ZIP, or JAR files

subcommands.name: -d
subcommands.description: Place the generated class files into the specified location. The location can be a directory, a ZIP, or a JAR file

subcommands.name: -include-runtime
subcommands.description: Include the Kotlin runtime into the resulting JAR file. Makes the resulting archive runnable on any Java-enabled environment

subcommands.name: -jdk-home
subcommands.description: Use a custom JDK home directory to include into the classpath if it differs from the default JAVA_HOME

subcommands.name: -jvm-target
subcommands.description: Specify the target version of the generated JVM bytecode. Possible values are 1.8, 9, 10, ..., 21. The default value is 1.8

subcommands.name: -java-parameters
subcommands.description: Generate metadata for Java 1.8 reflection on method parameters

subcommands.name: -module-name
subcommands.description: Set a custom name for the generated .kotlin_module file

subcommands.name: -no-jdk
subcommands.description: Don't automatically include the Java runtime into the classpath

subcommands.name: -no-reflect
subcommands.description: Don't automatically include the Kotlin reflection (kotlin-reflect.jar) into the classpath

subcommands.name: -no-stdlib
subcommands.description: Don't automatically include the Kotlin/JVM stdlib (kotlin-stdlib.jar) and Kotlin reflection (kotlin-reflect.jar) into the classpath

subcommands.name: -script-templates
subcommands.description: Script definition template classes. Use fully qualified class names and separate them with commas (,)
